⚖️ FEDERAL COURTS — Apply via OSCAR (oscar.uscourts.gov)
OSCAR — Online System for Clerkship Application and Review

CONFIRMED from oscar.uscourts.gov (primary source): Class of 2027: access Jan 20, 2026 (already granted). Class of 2028: access Jan 19, 2027 at 8 AM EST. Class of 2029: ~January 2028 (not yet announced — check oscar.uscourts.gov). Federal Hiring Plan application release date: June 8, 2026 (Class of 2027 2L apps). FlexJD: Use 'Alternate Graduation Date' path in OSCAR.
Federal Law Clerk Hiring Plan (Pilot): judges using the plan hold 2L apps until mid-June; 3L/grad apps released immediately. Non-plan judges: apply any time after OSCAR access date.
Typically requires prior Circuit clerkship; apply 2–3 years before start
Apply directly to each justice's chambers; deadlines unstated; typically hired via prior Circuit clerks network.
1–2 years before desired start; Hiring Plan dates apply for some judges
Volume of judges means more available clerkships than Circuit. Many judges actively recruit non-T14 candidates.
🌐 Bankruptcy clerk positions available in every district.
Often hire closer to start date than Article III courts. Excellent commercial law credential.
🌐 Magistrate judge clerkships are underutilized and excellent.
Often more accessible than Article III judges. Strong mentorship opportunities.
Apply 1 year before start; dedicated career page
Tax Court clerk ≠ tax clerk at a district court. Dedicated application through Tax Court's own system.
Unique jurisdiction covering claims against the federal government. Niche but prestigious.
Unique trade law credential. One of the most specialized federal courts.

Unique military law credential. Very few applications each year — strong odds for interested students.
Applications for Aug 2027 start: reviewed mid-June 2026; interviews July 2026
7th Circuit staff attorney positions posted on OSCAR. Applications reviewed mid-June 2026 for August 2027 starts.
